# FLOOR-PLAN PARSING PIPELINE — FINAL PRODUCTION-GRADE PANEL REPORT

*Integrated from 6 specialist drafts, with the skeptic's overrules applied and flagged. Adversarial pass complete. Every load-bearing 2024–2026 license/benchmark claim carries a source or a needs-verification + how-to-verify.*

---

## 1. EXECUTIVE VERDICT (the hard call)

Your plan is buildable in 8 weeks **only if you accept that the binding constraint is not the model — it is dataset licensing and evaluation integrity.** Almost every floor-plan dataset that matters is non-commercial (CubiCasa5K = CC BY-NC, FloorPlanCAD = CC BY-NC, ArchCAD-400K = NC+gated, Structured3D/ZInD/RPLAN = research-only), and the two you've been told are "commercial-OK" are not both confirmed: MSD's per-file license is unchecked and **ResPlan's dataset license is unverified — the paper is CC BY 4.0 but that does not license the data artifact.** The correct architecture is a **staged hybrid** — an input router splitting a deterministic vector-passthrough branch (born-digital PDFs) from a raster ML branch (scans), both converging on a **deterministic topology verifier** that emits the room-adjacency graph — not an end-to-end ViT, U-Net, or VLM. Your moat is the verifier plus a correction-loop data flywheel plus a proprietary messy-scan corpus you own; the segmentation backbone is commoditized. **Cerebras is not justified for this MVP** (verdict 4 of 4) — the only path that would change that is a VLM-vectorizer you've already deferred, making the Cerebras case circular. Ship one verifiable artifact (adjacency graph + navigable regions), lead your metrics with topology-validity-after-verifier and human-correction-minutes-per-plan — not pixel IoU — and label any robotics/AR output "preview/unverified."

---

## 2. DECISION TABLE

| Decision Area | Recommendation | Confidence | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| **MVP dataset mix** | **MSD** (graph-native, multi-unit, plausibly commercial — *verify repo LICENSE Wk1*) + **CubiCasa5K as research/pretrain ONLY** (CC BY-NC) + **owned ~1,100-plan labeled set**. ResPlan added **only if** its dataset-file license clears CC BY. | High | Only MSD is simultaneously commercial-clearable-as-data, graph-native, and commercial-adjacent. Owned data is the only license-clean real distribution. |
| **Gold benchmark dataset** | **Your own ~200-plan frozen, source-disjoint, messy-scan test set.** CubiCasa5K test split as secondary academic comparison only. | High | The only number you can publish/sell against without NC taint or leakage. |
| **Baseline architecture** | CubiCasa-style multi-task CNN (shared encoder → wall/room/icon heads). Research baseline floor. | High | Reproducible, cheap, gives walls+rooms+icons in one shot. NC — research only. |
| **Frontier architecture** | Vector branch: CADSpotting-class primitive panoptic spotter (87.4 PQ on FloorPlanCAD) for CAD; RoomFormer/PolyRoom two-level-query polygon decoder for raster→polygon. | Medium | Frontier numbers exist only on *vector/clean-polygon* data; do not assume they transfer to scans. |
| **Production architecture** | **Router → {deterministic vector passthrough ‖ raster multi-task seg + symbol detector + OCR-first text masking} → deterministic vectorizer → deterministic topology verifier → adjacency graph.** clDice as the one added wall-connectivity training loss. | High | Routes each input to its strongest path; hard topology guarantees; modular; debuggable in 8 weeks. |
| **Cerebras verdict** | **(4) Not justified for the MVP.** Conditional (3) path is circular (depends on the deferred VLM). Run the tiling-ablation topology benchmark before reconsidering. | High | Bottlenecks (data, topology, eval) are not compute-bound. CS-3 training is not self-serve for a small team. |
| **First 8-week wedge** | Vector passthrough branch + verifier + topology graph + owned messy-scan eval set, then raster baseline. Sell to **FM / CRE** (facility-management plan libraries). | High | Highest accuracy at lowest ML cost; cleanest demo; direct cost-replacement sale vs. offshore redraw. |
| **Biggest risk** | **Shipping a model trained on non-commercial data + inflating metrics via evaluation leakage.** | High | Either one ends a diligence meeting. Both are quiet, both are verified-real. |
| **Do-not-build-yet list** | Learned vector/VLM branch; Betti/PH losses; rich utility-symbol long tail; multi-floor stitching; AR/robotics/sim export with clearances; Cerebras; end-to-end graph transformer; GNN topology head. | High | None MVP-critical; each is a clean v2 epic. |

---

## 3. BIGGEST FLAWS IN THE CLIENT'S CURRENT PLAN (ranked)

1. **Treating "floor-plan parsing" as one problem.** It is two disjoint regimes — vector-native (CAD/born-digital PDF, where geometry is *ground truth*) and raster (scans/photos, where geometry must be *inferred*). Rasterizing a born-digital PDF to run a segmenter throws away the exact answer and reconstructs it worse. **Fix: route, don't unify.** Unify the *output schema*, not the model.

2. **Assuming the default datasets are usable for a commercial product.** They are mostly NC or academic-only (verified, §4). The plan implicitly trains a shippable model on data that legally blocks shipping. **This is the company-killer.**

3. **Treating ResPlan/MSD as confirmed commercial anchors.** Three drafts upgraded ResPlan from "needs verification" to "commercial-OK" — *the paper's CC BY 4.0 does not license the dataset artifact* (the exact CubiCasa repo-vs-data trap). MSD's per-file license is also unchecked. **Until the dataset LICENSE files are read, both are research-only by default.**

4. **Believing topology falls out of a good segmenter.** A model can score 90 mIoU and still emit a topologically broken graph (a 1px wall gap merges two rooms; a door links nothing). For robotics/digital-twin buyers, topology errors are catastrophic and IoU is nearly blind to them. **Topology must be a deterministic verifier, not a softmax you trust.**

5. **Setting clean-data SOTA numbers (0.85–0.92 IoU) as messy-scan MVP targets.** The 92.5% figure is *external-wall* IoU on *clean born-vector* FPBench-2K (FloorplanVLM); CubiCasa's own real-scan baseline is ~57.5% room IoU. **Promising 0.85 across the real distribution fails review.**

6. **Assuming synthetic/augmentation solves real-world messiness.** Corruption-augmentation (the best idea in the panel) fixes the raster-*noise* axis only. It cannot turn a Swiss apartment into a US commercial office with a fire-egress legend. **Content/regional/commercial domain shift stays fully open — only owned real data closes it.**

7. **Leaning on Cerebras to look ambitious.** The only Cerebras justification depends on a VLM architecture every draft defers — circular. **Cut it from the critical path.**

8. **Evaluation leakage is named but never gated.** Same-building near-duplicates and cross-customer plan-of-record overlap inflate every number, and source-splitting alone does not catch the cross-customer leak.

---

## 4. DATASET MATRIX (ranked) + forced answers

Ranking = production value for a *commercially shippable* CV+topology pipeline. License risk is weighted heavily because it gates revenue.

| # | Dataset | Size / Type | License (verified status) | Commercial? | Best use | Key weakness |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **1** | **MSD** (Modified Swiss Dwellings, ECCV 2024) | ~5.3K multi-apartment / 18.9K apts; raster seg + **typed room-graph** | Repo/paper exist; **per-file dataset LICENSE needs verification** — open [github.com/caspervanengelenburg/msd](https://github.com/caspervanengelenburg/msd) LICENSE | Likely yes (verify) | **MVP topology anchor**; commercial-clearable finetune; topology eval | Swiss-only; clean BIM-derived (not scanned); sparse symbols |
| **2** | **CubiCasa5K** | 5,000 plans, raster + SVG, 80+ categories | **CC BY-NC 4.0 — NON-COMMERCIAL, verified** ([LICENSE](https://github.com/CubiCasa/CubiCasa5k/blob/master/LICENSE)) | **NO** | **Pretrain + research benchmark ONLY**; symbol-taxonomy reference | NC blocks shipping; Finnish style; residential; ~57.5% room-IoU baseline ([arXiv 1904.01920](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1904.01920)) |
| **3** | **ResPlan** (arXiv 2508.14006, Aug 2025) | 17,000 residential; raster + vector + **adjacency graph** | Paper CC BY 4.0; **dataset-artifact license UNVERIFIED** — find LICENSE in the dataset repo before any commercial use | **Unconfirmed — treat as research-only until verified** | Scale-up residential graph finetune *if* license clears | New/unproven; license unconfirmed; residential only |
| **4** | **FloorPlanCAD** (ICCV'21) | 15,663 vector CAD drawings, 35 classes, panoptic | **CC BY-NC 4.0 — NON-COMMERCIAL, verified** ([floorplancad.github.io](https://floorplancad.github.io/)) | **NO** | Research symbol-spotter pretrain; commercial-symbol taxonomy design | NC; vector-only (no scan noise); abandoned project (link-rot risk) |
| **5** | **ArchCAD-400K** (arXiv 2503.22346, Mar 2025) | 413K chunks / 5,538 drawings, 86% commercial | **NON-COMMERCIAL + gated, verified** ([HF jackluoluo/ArchCAD](https://huggingface.co/datasets/jackluoluo/ArchCAD)) — "Open" in title is misleading | **NO** | Research commercial-symbol pretrain (best commercial CAD coverage) | NC+gated → research only; vector CAD, not scans |
| **6** | **Raster-to-Graph dataset** (CGF 2024) | ~10K+ residential, junction+segment **structural graph** | Per-repo, **needs verification** ([github.com/SizheHu/Raster-to-Graph](https://github.com/SizheHu/Raster-to-Graph)) | Verify | Closest to your output schema; topology training/eval | License unverified; residential |
| **7** | **Structured3D** (ECCV'20) | 3,500 synthetic houses | **Research/educational only, verified** ([GitHub bertjiazheng](https://github.com/bertjiazheng/Structured3D)) | **NO** | **Pretrain ONLY; never eval** | Synthetic domain gap (closed doors, single-pano); NC |
| **8** | **RPLAN** | 80,788 plans (Asia) | **No redistribution; research-only, verified** | **NO** | Generative/layout pretrain only | Schematic; no-redistribute; not parsing-grade GT |
| **9** | **ZInD** (Zillow) | ~1,575 homes / 71K panos | **Academic-only; no commercial, verified** ([github.com/zillow/zind](https://github.com/zillow/zind)) | **NO** | Layout/connectivity eval (research) | Pano-centric, not document plans; US-only |
| **X** | **LIFULL HOME'S** | 5.3M images | **Academic-only; private companies explicitly rejected, verified** ([NII IDR](https://www.nii.ac.jp/dsc/idr/en/lifull/)) | **NO** | Do not touch as a startup | Application from a company "will not be accepted" |
| eval | **FPBench-2K** | 2,000 test (1,040 Manhattan / 960 non-Manhattan) | Per-paper, **needs verification** ([arXiv 2602.06507](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.06507)) | eval | Adopt as non-Manhattan eval split | Born-vector-derived; not messy scans |

**FORCED ANSWERS:**

- **MVP anchor → MSD** (pending Wk1 repo-LICENSE check). CubiCasa5K = research/pretrain workhorse, never the anchor. *Skeptic overrule applied: Draft 4's "FloorPlanCAD #1" rejected (NC, vector-only); Draft 5's "train shippable model on CubiCasa+ResPlan" rejected — CubiCasa is verified CC BY-NC, Draft 5's "MIT" was the repo-vs-data trap.*
- **Minimum manual-label set → ~1,100 plans**, composed: 400 residential scanned PDFs · 350 commercial/multi-unit (stairs, elevators, utility symbols, legends) · 150 phone-photo/low-res/rotated/cropped · 100 paired CAD-export+rasterization · 100 hard-negative/heavy-text-overlay. Split 70/10/20 train/val/**frozen test**. The **~220-plan frozen test set is the credibility centerpiece** — license-clean, distribution-matched, never trained on.
- **Licensing blockers (hard, research-only):** CubiCasa5K, FloorPlanCAD, ArchCAD-400K, Structured3D, ZInD, RPLAN, LIFULL. Get a written legal ruling on whether NC-pretrained weights can seed a commercial finetune — **treat as unsafe-to-ship until cleared.**
- **What to synthesize → corruption, not content.** Apply scan noise, JPEG, rotation/crop, text/dimension overlays, furniture clutter *on top of clean vector GT*. Do **not** synthesize layouts (Structured3D proves synthetic geometry has a documented modality gap). **Caveat (skeptic): corruption-aug closes the noise axis only; the content/regional/commercial axis needs owned real data.**
- **What to hold out → your owned ~220-plan frozen messy-scan set.** Never train on it; vault it so it never leaks.

---

## 5. RECOMMENDED ARCHITECTURE

| Role | Pick |
|---|---|
| **Baseline** | CubiCasa-style multi-task CNN (shared encoder → wall/room/icon heads). Research floor only (NC). |
| **Frontier** | Vector: CADSpotting/SymPoint primitive panoptic spotter (PQ 83–87 on FloorPlanCAD). Raster→polygon: RoomFormer/PolyRoom two-level-query decoder. |
| **Production (SHIP)** | **Router → {vector passthrough ‖ raster multi-task seg + symbol detector + OCR-first text masking} → deterministic vectorizer (skeleton/junction-snap/gap-heal) → deterministic topology verifier → adjacency graph + navigable regions.** OCR = PaddleOCR (handles rotated text natively), run **first** to mask text out of the wall channel. |
| **AVOID** | Standalone ViT/Swin as a "solution"; rasterizing born-digital CAD then segmenting it; one end-to-end model for both regimes; a *learned* topology verifier in v1; any pipeline trained mainly on CubiCasa5K shipped commercially. |
| **TEST FIRST (Wk1–2)** | (1) Input-router accuracy (vector vs raster, scanned vs embedded-text) — **gate ≥98%**; (2) raster backbone bake-off (SegFormer-B2/B3 vs Swin vs HRNet) on a messy held-out set; (3) tiling/stitch wall-continuity loss. |
| **SHIP in 8 weeks** | Vector passthrough + raster panoptic core + deterministic vectorizer + deterministic topology verifier + OCR room-label fusion + JSON/graph export. |
| **DEFER (v2+)** | Learned vector branch (CADSpotting/SymPoint training), RoomFormer/PolyRoom training, VLM-vectorizer, Betti/PH losses, learned verifier, stairs/elevator multi-floor stitching, AR/sim export, legend parsing, full utility-symbol taxonomy. |

The one added training loss worth the risk: **clDice** (CVPR 2021) on the wall mask — it directly attacks the 1px-gap room-leak by preserving thin-structure connectivity. Betti/persistent-homology losses are unstable; keep them out of v1.

---

## 6. CEREBRAS-SPECIFIC VERDICT

> **(4) NOT JUSTIFIED for the MVP.**

**Justification.** Cerebras is two products. CS-3/WSE-3 *training* (the only part on-topic for CV — documented tiling-free U-Net up to 5k×5k, [Cerebras high-res CV blog](https://www.cerebras.ai/blog/unlocking-high-resolution-computer-vision-with-wafer-scale-technology)) is **not self-serve** for a small team in an 8-week window (enterprise/ALCF allocation — needs verification of your access). The Cerebras *Inference* API serves LLMs/VLMs only — irrelevant unless your model *is* a VLM. Your hardest problems — annotation quality, schema unification, topology correctness, scan-noise domain shift, leakage-free evaluation — are **not compute-bound.** The genuine wafer-scale win (tiling-free high-res training, which removes one class of tile-seam topology break) is real but GPUs handle it adequately via overlap-tile + sliding-window.

**The conditional (3) path is circular.** It rests entirely on adopting a **VLM-as-vectorizer** (FloorplanVLM-style, ~3,000 autoregressive tokens/plan) where Cerebras inference latency would matter — but that architecture is the *deferred* path in every draft. A justification that depends on the thing you've decided not to build is not a verdict.

**What to benchmark to prove it (run before spending a dollar):** the **tiling-ablation topology test.** Train one architecture two ways — (A) GPU 512² overlap-tile + sliding-window stitch; (B) WSE-3 whole-image at ≥4k². Evaluate on large commercial plans with a **topology metric** (room-adjacency graph-edit-distance + room-leak rate + wall-junction F1), not pixel IoU. **Cerebras is justified iff (B) reduces graph-edit-distance / room-leak by ≥3–5 pts absolute, surviving annotation noise.** If the gap is <2 pts, you proved you don't need it.

---

## 7. TRAINING RECIPE (Stage 0–6)

**Stage 0 — Canonical ontology + normalization + leakage firewall (BUILD FIRST, Wk1–2).** Define one canonical ontology (wall, door, window, opening, room[+type], stairs, elevator, column, utility-symbol{N}, label-text). Build per-dataset mapping tables (CubiCasa ~80 → canonical; FloorPlanCAD 35; ArchCAD 56; MSD room-types) but keep a fine-grained "native-class" field so you train coarse-robust + fine-when-labels-agree. Normalize resolution (resample to fixed px-per-meter), **recover scale from dimension-string OCR** (a feature, not noise), deskew, canonicalize rotation. **Leakage firewall: split by building/source, never by image; freeze a per-domain held-out set (residential/commercial/scanned-noisy) that no synthetic or augmented data ever enters.** *Forced call: this unglamorous stage is the highest-leverage work; do it before any training.*

**Stage 1 — Synthetic pretrain (Structured3D).** PRETRAIN-ONLY. Initialize geometric priors; never mix at high ratio into final finetune, **never in eval.**

**Stage 2 — CAD/vector pretrain (FloorPlanCAD + ArchCAD-400K, research-only).** **Forced call: BOTH rasterize-for-backbone AND train-native-on-primitives.** Deployment inputs are raster, so the backbone must be raster; but native-vector gives the symbol head clean line-grained supervision. Rasterize with multiple render styles (line weights, dash patterns, with/without dimension text) to pre-bake style robustness. NC → research weights only.

**Stage 3 — Real raster finetune (CubiCasa5K + scan-noise augmentation, research) → owned-set finetune (shippable).** Augmentation = real-degradation pipeline (JPEG, scan blur, skew, furniture-clutter overlays, text/dimension-string overlays, legend boxes, partial crops). Class imbalance: per-class loss weighting (effective-number-of-samples) + copy-paste for rare symbols + separate symbol-detection head with its own sampler. **The shippable weights are finetuned on the owned + (verified-commercial) data only.**

**Stage 4 — Topology-aware refinement (the part Cerebras can't help, and the part that wins).** Topology enforced by a **deterministic post-processor**, not learned end-to-end: wall-junction graph with snap/merge; closed-loop room polygons with flood-fill leak-detection and repair; room-adjacency graph (nodes=rooms, edges=shared-wall + door/opening); connectivity/navigability via door/opening/stair/elevator edges. MSD graph GT (and ResPlan, if licensed) supervises and *evaluates* the adjacency head.

**Stage 5 — Domain adaptation + active learning + human correction (spend your owned-data budget here).** Run Stage-0–4 on real target plans; surface low-confidence / topology-inconsistent cases via active learning; humans correct; fold back. Mine hard negatives (legends, title-blocks, dimension strings, furniture mislabeled as walls/symbols) and re-weight. **This loop produces your commercially-licensed owned set — the moat and the path off the NC island.**

**Stage 6 — Production eval + regression.** Topology-first metrics (§8). Freeze per-domain eval sets; every model change runs the full topology suite; **block merges that regress room-leak or graph-edit-distance, or reintroduce any frozen HARD failure.**

---

## 8. LOSSES, EVALUATION, AND VERIFIER STRATEGY

### Loss stack (raster track)
`L = 1.0·CE + 1.0·Dice + 0.5·Focal(symbols) + ramp(boundary 0→1.0) + 0.3·clDice + 1.0·junction_heatmap + 0.5·junction_offset`
**Curriculum (do not turn everything on at step 0):** Phase 1 CE+Dice+junctions → Phase 2 add focal + boundary ramp → Phase 3 add clDice → optional Phase 4 PH fine-tune (low weight, late). clDice is the single highest-leverage addition (room-leak killer). **Vector track:** per-primitive CE + instance/grouping (contrastive/matching) + line-segment (endpoint L1 + angle/length) + PQ-aligned matching + adjacency BCE (ResPlan-supervised, if licensed).

### Topological verifier spec (this is the product)
Runs post-model on the assembled output; returns structured pass/warn/fail; rejects structurally-invalid plans **even when IoU looks good.**

| Check | HARD / WARN | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Room polygon validity (self-intersect, non-closure, zero/negative area) | **HARD** | Non-closed room is unusable for sim/nav |
| **Room-leak (flood-fill escape; β₀ region-count error)** | **HARD** | *The* failure IoU hides; two merged rooms = robot-fatal |
| Betti β₀ / β₁ error | β₀ **HARD**, β₁ **WARN** | β₀ = leak/spurious-split; β₁ often legit courtyard |
| Door-to-wall consistency (endpoint on a wall) | **HARD** | Floating door = broken connectivity graph |
| Door connectivity (each door links exactly two distinct rooms / room↔exterior) | **HARD** | This *is* the navigability guarantee |
| **Scale/units sanity from dimension strings** | **HARD** (skeptic overrule — was WARN) | Wrong px-per-meter silently garbages every navigable region |
| Window-to-wall | WARN (HARD if on no wall) | Windows matter less for nav |
| Room-adjacency consistency | WARN | Graph errors degrade but aren't always fatal |
| Planar-graph validity (no dangles > ε, no overlaps) | HARD (dangles) / WARN (overlaps) | Dangling walls break vectorization/meshing |

**The verifier feeds the flywheel:** every HARD-fail → `hard_negatives/` pool labeled by failed check → up-weighted next finetune; each fix frozen as a named regression case → CI gate (a model reintroducing a frozen HARD failure cannot promote); verifier report surfaces in-product as a clickable defect list → drives down human-correction minutes; pass-with-high-confidence plans become pseudo-label candidates, fails become human-labeling priority.

### Eval framework + target critique (with better numbers)
Always report **sliced**: residential vs commercial · born-digital vs scanned · clean vs low-quality. A single aggregate is a leakage/marketing artifact. **Split by source/building, never image. Vault the messy eval set so it never touches training; perceptual-hash dedup before any corrected example enters train; a discovered leak is a do-not-ship event.**

| Client target | Reality | Better number (frozen messy-scan set) |
|---|---|---|
| Wall IoU ≥0.85 | Clean only; ~0.57–0.75 on scans | **≥0.75 messy; reserve ≥0.85 for clean born-digital slice** |
| Room IoU ≥0.85 | CubiCasa mean 57.5% ([1904.01920](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1904.01920)) | **≥0.70 messy mean; ≥0.80 clean; per-class floors for corridors/closets** |
| Door/window F1 ≥0.80 | Generic detectors ~45–46% mAP on real doors | **≥0.65 messy; track door-*connectivity* correctness over box mAP** |
| Symbol mAP ≥0.80 | CAD PQ leaderboard 59.5→87.4 — *vector PQ, not raster box-mAP* | **F1 ≥0.60 messy (honest); defer the long tail; 87.4 PQ is NOT your number** |
| Near-zero room leaks | Right goal, wrong layer | **Leak ≤5% (HARD do-not-ship gate), enforced by verifier** |
| Adjacency-graph accuracy TBD | Set it | **Edge-F1 ≥0.80; navigable-region reachability ≥90%** |
| "92.5% wall IoU" (FloorplanVLM) | **External-wall IoU on clean FPBench-2K, single-source, unreplicated** ([arXiv 2602.06507](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.06507)) | Target to chase, not a baseline to assume |

**Headline metrics for the deck:** topology-validity-after-verifier (**≥0.97 clean / ≥0.90 messy**) and **median human-correction minutes/plan** — not pixel IoU.

---

## 9. DATA AUGMENTATION AND ROBUSTNESS PLAN (matrix)

Phase: **PT** pretrain · **FT** finetune · **RT** robustness-test-only.

| Aug | Failure mode | Phase | Caution |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Broken lines / micro-gaps** | #1 leak cause | FT (targeted) | **Highest-leverage; pair with clDice.** Don't overdo → teaches hallucinated walls |
| **Dimension strings crossing walls** | numbers break wall continuity | FT (targeted) | High value, under-modeled; pair with broken-line aug |
| Text overlays | labels/notes over geometry | FT | Use real fonts/dim strings; random text is unrealistic |
| Line thinning/thickening | varying wall weights | PT+FT | High value — weight varies hugely across offices |
| Furniture clutter | residential mess | FT | Composite real symbols; verify walls survive |
| Crop / tiling | large commercial plans | PT+FT (required) | Stitch-aware so rooms aren't cut blindly |
| Scan noise / JPEG / blur / contrast | scan & phone-photo degradation | FT | Match real scanner profiles; mild blur only |
| Rotation / skew / perspective | scanner skew, photographed plans | PT+FT | Small angles for perspective; don't let rotation dominate Manhattan priors |
| Scale variation | DPI differences | PT+FT | Tie to physical scale if known |
| Stamps / title-blocks / legends | corner blocks occluding plan | FT | Composite real crops; geometry inside legend bbox excluded |
| Regional / CAD-template style | domain shift (Finnish→US, office blocks) | FT | Aug can't fully fake it — **acquire real regional data** |
| Handwriting / partial plans | markup scans, cropped/multi-page | RT (+some FT) | Hard to synthesize realistically → keep as eval |

**Guardrails:** never augment so hard your own verifier would reject the augmented GT; validate aug realism against the real stress set (feature-distance); **augmentation sets the floor, real messy data sets the launch metric — never report a launch number on augmented data; and corruption-aug never closes content/regional shift (skeptic).**

---

## 10. TOP FAILURE MODES AND MITIGATIONS

S = severity 1–5. VER = verifier mitigation.

| # | Failure mode | S | Algorithmic mitigation | VER / data / UX |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Text blocks walls (labels/dims over lines) | 5 | OCR-first text masking; re-link walls across gap | Require wall continuity post-mask; synth text overlays |
| 2 | Micro-gaps in walls (1–3px) | 5 | Morphological close + junction snap within ε(wall-thickness); clDice | Close loops; reject open faces; train on degraded copies |
| 3 | Door symbol read as wall break | 5 | Detector tags opening; treat gap as typed edge | Opening must connect exactly two rooms |
| 4 | Furniture mistaken as structural | 4 | Separate furniture class; exclude from wall graph | Furniture must lie inside a room, never bound it |
| 5 | Dashed lines parsed as walls | 4 | Dash-period classifier → annotation layer | Dashed excluded from load-bearing graph unless opt-in |
| 6 | Low-res scans (<150 DPI) | 5 | Super-res preprocess; adaptive ε | **Warn + request higher-DPI rescan**; multi-res train |
| 7 | Thick walls merge two rooms | 4 | Centerline (skeleton) extraction, not fill | Split merged face along medial axis; flag area outliers |
| 8 | Thin walls disappear | 5 | Preserve native res for wall channel | Insert candidate wall between two labeled rooms; prompt user |
| 9 | Non-Manhattan / slanted / curved | 4 | Arc fitting; don't snap-to-grid blindly | Train/eval on FPBench-2K non-Manhattan split |
| 10 | Commercial repeated units (hotel/office) | 4 | MSD inter-unit connectivity priors | Verify each unit closes; check shared-wall consistency |
| 11 | Mixed-scale single page (inset + main) | 4 | Plan-region detection; per-region scale | Reject single global scale; require per-region px/m |
| 12 | Stairs/elevators misread as rooms | 4 | Dedicated stair/elevator detector | Room containing stair glyph → circulation node |
| 13 | Title-blocks / legends interfere | 4 | Plan-region detection crops frame | Geometry inside legend bbox excluded |
| 14 | Multi-page PDFs | 3 | Page classifier (plan vs section/elevation) | Each page → independent graph; link floors via stair/elevator |
| 15 | Rotated / skewed | 4 | Hough/projection deskew; PaddleOCR residual rotation | Re-check after deskew; rotation augmentation |
| 16 | Partial / cropped plans | 3 | Detect boundary-touching dangles; don't force-close | Mark boundary-open rooms "incomplete"; request full scan |
| 17 | CAD layer artifacts (construction/hatch/grid) | 4 | Filter by layer/lineweight/color | Verify filtered geometry still closes |
| 18 | OCR labels mistaken for geometry | 4 | OCR-first masking | Text-region pixels never enter wall graph |
| 19 | Symbols overlapping wall lines | 3 | Detect symbol, restore wall beneath | Wall continuity enforced under symbol footprint |
| 20 | **Scale-bar / dimension misread** | 5 | Cross-check OCR'd dims vs pixel distances | **HARD gate: reject scale if disagreement > tolerance** |
| 21 | **Cross-customer plan-of-record eval leak** | 5 | Perceptual-hash dedup across the train/test wall | **Do-not-ship event if discovered; vault the eval set** |

---

## 11. PRODUCTION PIPELINE

(See the diagram above for the routed flow.) Per stage: **[M]** model · **[R]** rule · **[VER]** verifier · **[H]** human.

| Stage | Approach | Editable | API-exposed | Feeds training? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Ingestion / page-split / decrypt | [R] | no | upload | no |
| Page classification (plan vs section/legend) | [M] | yes | page list | **yes** |
| Plan-region detection (crop title-block/legend) | [M]+[R] | yes | region boxes | yes |
| Deskew / dewarp / DPI-normalize | [R] | yes (rotate) | — | no |
| Scale recovery (dimension-string OCR) | [M] | confirm scale | scale field | **yes** |
| Branch select (vector vs raster) | [R] | — | — | no |
| **Vector branch** (PyMuPDF get_drawings → layer filter) | [R] | — | — | no |
| **Raster branch** (multi-task seg: walls/rooms/openings) | [M] | via UI | — | **yes** |
| OCR (run FIRST, mask text) | [M] | yes | labels | **yes** |
| Symbol detection (doors/windows/stairs/elevators/utility) | [M] | yes | symbols | **yes** |
| Vectorization (skeleton/snap/gap-heal) | [R]+[VER] | yes (drag) | geometry | no |
| **Topology graph** (planar faces → rooms; openings → edges) | [VER] | yes | **primary output** | no |
| **Verifier** (close loops, repair, enforce invariants) | [VER] | — | validity flags | **yes (failures → hard negatives)** |
| Confidence scoring | [M]+[R] | — | confidence | yes (calibration) |
| Human-review loop (route low-conf/failed) | [H] | — | review queue | **yes (highest-value signal)** |
| Correction UI (drag walls, retype rooms, fix openings) | [H] | the point | — | **yes** |
| Training-feedback (corrections → labels, dedup/leak-check) | [R] | — | — | **yes** |
| Audit log / model versioning / regression suite | [R]+[VER] | no | audit/version | no |

**API contract (sim/robotics/AR/digital-twin):** room-adjacency graph, connectivity topology, navigable regions, vectorized geometry, room labels, typed openings, per-element confidence, model version. **Storage:** raw input (hashed), all intermediate annotations, corrections, verifier repair logs (immutable audit). **Leakage guard is part of the pipeline, not an afterthought.**

---

## 12. 8-WEEK IMPLEMENTATION PLAN

| Wk | Goal | Must | Should | Stretch | Defer |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **1** | Licenses + ingestion + router | Clear MSD/ResPlan/Raster-to-Graph **dataset** LICENSEs; legal ruling on NC-pretrain→commercial-finetune; ingestion + **vector-layer detector**; freeze eval split (source-disjoint) | Page/region triage classifier | — | CAD learned branch |
| **2** | Normalization + label schema + verifier v1 | Deskew/DPI-normalize/text-strip; lock ontology; **verifier v1** (validity, leak, door-to-wall) on hand-made plans; start labeling 150–300 gold plans; **gate router ≥98%** | Backbone bake-off (SegFormer-B3/Swin/HRNet); IAA ≥0.8 | — | Multi-floor |
| **3** | Raster baseline | Train seg on MSD(+pretrain CubiCasa, research) → finetune owned; door/stair detector; **report on YOUR real held-out** | Class-imbalance handling; scan-noise aug | — | ArchCAD (license-gated) |
| **4** | Structural + OCR | OCR room-labels + dimension strings; **scale inference (HARD gate)**; attach labels to polygons | Metric-scale estimator; symbol substitution caution | — | symbol-substitution library |
| **5** | Vectorization + adjacency graph | Skeleton→line-fit→polygonize + corner-snap; **room-adjacency + connectivity graph**; door-to-room linking | clDice + broken-line aug | — | learned vectorizer |
| **6** | Verifier + confidence (the moat) | Rule engine (closure, room-without-door, disconnected region, scale sanity); per-element confidence; **hard-negative mining live** | Confidence calibration | — | GNN topology head |
| **7** | Human-correction loop | Correction UI (drag walls, retype, fix openings) + export (GeoJSON/SVG/graph JSON); **time-to-correct KPI** | Active-learning worst-confidence queue | — | AR/digital-twin export |
| **8** | Feedback proof + demo | Retrain on Wk7 corrections; **measured Δ ≥3–5pt F1** on held-out; freeze 30-plan never-trained demo set; live demo on 5 unseen messy plans | Topology dashboard; do-not-ship enforcement | end-to-end graph transformer (only if ahead) | VLM, Cerebras |

---

## 13. STARTUP / INVESTMENT CRITIQUE

**Wedge.** Not "parse any floor plan." It is **"turn a messy PDF/scan into a clean, verified, editable vector + room graph in minutes, with a human-in-the-loop that gets cheaper every week."** The wedge is the **verifier + correction-loop**, not raw accuracy (commoditizing — open weights + CubiCasa reach ~0.8 on clean data).

**First customer (ranked):** (1) **Facility management / IWMS/CAFM** — sit on thousands of legacy PDF plans they *must* digitize; severe, recurring, budgeted pain; buyer = FM/RE-ops director. (2) **CRE / leasing** — BOMA area calc, stacking plans; pays per-plan. (3) **Scan-to-BIM / insurance service shops** — you cut their unit cost. (4) **Robotics / digital-twin** — exciting but *worst* first customer: long cycle, demands metric+clearance correctness you won't have at MVP → pilot, don't anchor.

**Defensibility.** Commoditized: wall/room seg, OCR, "parse CubiCasa." Defensible: (a) **proprietary labeled corpus of real messy plans** accumulated via the correction loop (compounds); (b) the **verifier / topology-correctness layer**; (c) **vector-native passthrough done well**; (d) workflow lock-in inside a customer's plan library.

**Most compelling demo.** Drag in a rotated, scanned, furniture-cluttered PDF → <60s editable vector + colored adjacency graph → make one correction → verifier flags a missing door → click retrain → next plan improves. The **feedback-improves-it moment** separates you from a static model demo.

**Benchmark that proves superiority.** Not CubiCasa F1 (everyone's at ~0.86; clean and leakage-prone). Lead with a held-out messy real benchmark: **(1) topology/adjacency edge-F1, (2) % rooms reachable, (3) human time-to-correct, (4) Δ-accuracy per feedback cycle.** The last two are what a buyer and investor actually price.

**Reject-reasons (what kills the raise):** training a shippable model on CC-BY-NC data; reporting numbers on a leaked split; anchoring on robotics/AR before a boring-but-paying FM/CRE wedge; "end-to-end model solves messiness" with no human loop and no verifier; synthetic-data hand-wave.

**Business model.** Land **usage-based per-plan** (matches the offshore-redraw cost-replacement frame buyers already understand) → expand to **seat/library SaaS** for the correction tooling → **API** for the proptech/robotics ecosystem. The corrected-corpus moat funds a future accuracy lead you license.

**Biggest adoption blocker:** customers won't replace their *authoritative plan-of-record* with output they can't trust — which is exactly why the **verifier + correction loop is the product, not a feature.**

---

## 14. OPEN QUESTIONS THAT MATERIALLY AFFECT THE ANSWER

1. **Does the MSD per-file dataset LICENSE permit commercial use?** (The forced anchor — unchecked by all six drafts.) Verify: [github.com/caspervanengelenburg/msd](https://github.com/caspervanengelenburg/msd) LICENSE.
2. **Is the ResPlan dataset artifact (not the paper) CC BY 4.0?** Verify: find the LICENSE in the dataset repo linked from [arXiv 2508.14006](https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.14006). *If unconfirmed, it is research-only — do not anchor on it.*
3. **Can NC-pretrained weights legally seed a commercial finetune?** (Unsettled; gates whether *any* rich dataset is usable even for pretraining a shipped model.) Get written legal sign-off.
4. **Born-digital vs scanned mix in your real input distribution?** Determines whether the vector branch is the main product or an edge case.
5. **Can you get WSE-3 *training* allocation in 8 weeks?** If not, the entire Cerebras "training" column is theoretical (collapses verdict to a hard 4). Verify via Cerebras sales / ALCF.
6. **Does the first customer need commercial symbols (elevators, fire/utility legends) on day one, or residential-first?** Determines label-budget allocation in the ~1,100-plan set.
7. **Are FPBench-2K / FloorplanVLM's 92.5% reproducible and leakage-free?** Verify the eval protocol in [arXiv 2602.06507](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.06507) before citing in any deck (Feb-2026 preprint, single-source, treat skeptically).

---

## 15. FINAL HARD RECOMMENDATION

**MVP wedge.** A verified messy-PDF/scan → clean editable vector + room-adjacency graph in minutes, with a correction loop that compounds into a proprietary dataset. Sell the *trust* (verifier) and the *cost-replacement* (vs offshore redraw), not the model.

**Customer.** Facility-management / IWMS plan libraries first. CRE second. Robotics/AR is a pilot, never the anchor.

**Dataset mix.** Train shippable weights on **MSD** (verify LICENSE Wk1) **+ your owned ~1,100-plan labeled set** (+ ResPlan only if its dataset license clears CC BY). Use CubiCasa5K / FloorPlanCAD / ArchCAD-400K / Structured3D **for research/pretraining experiments only** — all verified non-commercial. Benchmark on your **~220-plan frozen, source-disjoint, messy-scan set.**

**Architecture.** Router → {deterministic vector passthrough ‖ raster multi-task seg + symbol detector + OCR-first text masking} → deterministic vectorizer → **deterministic topology verifier** → adjacency graph + navigable regions. clDice as the one added wall-connectivity loss. Not ViT, not U-Net-alone, not VLM, not end-to-end.

**Metrics (frozen messy-scan set).** Topology-validity-after-verifier ≥0.90 messy / ≥0.97 clean (headline) · room-leak ≤5% (HARD) · door-connectivity ≥90% · adjacency edge-F1 ≥0.80 · navigable-region reachability ≥90% · wall IoU ≥0.75 messy / ≥0.85 clean-born-digital · room IoU ≥0.70 messy · scale ±5% (HARD) · median human-correction <5 min/plan · Δ ≥3–5pt F1 from one feedback cycle.

**Do-not-ship criteria (any one).** HARD verifier-fail rate >2% on gold · invalid-polygon >1% · a frozen regression HARD failure reintroduced · scale-gate failure · median correction >10 min/plan · a discovered train/test leak · weights derived from NC data without legal clearance.

**Demo.** Rotated, cluttered, scanned PDF → <60s editable vector + colored adjacency graph → one correction → verifier flags a missing door → retrain → next plan improves.

**8-week roadmap.** Wk1–2 licenses + router + verifier-v1 + eval-freeze; Wk3–4 raster baseline + OCR/scale; Wk5 vectorization + adjacency graph; Wk6 verifier + hard-negative mining; Wk7 correction UI; Wk8 measured-feedback proof + demo.

**Post-MVP.** Learned vector/VLM branch (the SOTA-chasing upgrade and the *only* place Cerebras enters), Betti/PH losses, multi-floor stitching, AR/sim/robotics export with clearances, full utility-symbol taxonomy — each a clean v2 epic with its own eval.

**Top-5 risks.** (1) Shipping NC-data-derived weights. (2) Evaluation leakage inflating every number. (3) ResPlan/MSD license assumed-not-verified. (4) Clean-data SOTA numbers promised on messy scans. (5) Three-products-in-a-trenchcoat scope creep (residential + commercial + robotics).

**Top-5 next-week actions.** (1) Open and read the **MSD and ResPlan dataset LICENSE files**; segregate train-vs-benchmark by license in a manifest. (2) Get a written legal ruling on NC-pretrain→commercial-finetune. (3) Build the **input router** and gate it ≥98%; stand up the vector passthrough branch. (4) Start labeling the owned set and **freeze the ~220-plan messy-scan test split on day one** (vaulted, perceptual-hash dedup). (5) Build **verifier v1** (validity + leak + door-to-wall + scale gate) against hand-made plans.

**What NOT to build.** No end-to-end ViT/U-Net/VLM. No learned topology verifier in v1. No rich utility-symbol long tail. No multi-floor stitching. No AR/sim/robotics export with clearances. No Cerebras dependency. No model trained on CubiCasa5K/FloorPlanCAD/ArchCAD/Structured3D shipped commercially. **Build the router, the verifier, the correction loop, and the owned dataset — everything else is deferrable.**

---

*Load-bearing licenses verified this pass: CubiCasa5K = CC BY-NC 4.0 ([LICENSE](https://github.com/CubiCasa/CubiCasa5k/blob/master/LICENSE)); FloorPlanCAD = CC BY-NC ([floorplancad.github.io](https://floorplancad.github.io/)); ArchCAD-400K = non-commercial + gated ([HF](https://huggingface.co/datasets/jackluoluo/ArchCAD)); Structured3D / ZInD / RPLAN / LIFULL = research-only ([NII IDR](https://www.nii.ac.jp/dsc/idr/en/lifull/)). Needs-verification before budget: MSD per-file license, ResPlan dataset-artifact license (paper CC BY ≠ data), Raster-to-Graph + FPBench-2K licenses, NC-pretrain→commercial-finetune legality, WSE-3 training allocation, FloorplanVLM ([arXiv 2602.06507](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.06507)) eval protocol/leakage.*
